subject: [RFC PATCH v2 7/7] mm, swap: widen swap_info_struct max/pages to unsigned long

reviews.author: Nhat Pham
reviews.summary: Raised a concern that failing swapoff due to IO failure might not be necessary, as data is likely lost anyway. Also pointed out an inconsistency in existing code where atomic_long_t is used but the 30th bit is set.
reviews.raw_body:
On Sun, Jun 14, 2026 at 10:23 PM Nhat Pham <nphamcs@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> > #define SWAP_USAGE_OFFLIST_BIT (1UL << (BITS_PER_TYPE(atomic_t) - 2))
>
> Technically it's already a bit weird in existing code lol. The type of
> inuse_page is atomic_long_t already, but we use the 30th bit, which
> contradicts comment "Use the second highest bit of inuse_pages
> counter". It's only incidentally correct because the range of the swap
> device never reaches this high.

to clarify a bit - I meant to say the range of the swap devices never
reach this high in real system that I have seen, not that it's not
possible even theoretically.

[...]

> >
> > Could this cause try_to_unuse() to abort prematurely thinking the device is
> > empty, causing sys_swapoff() to forcefully tear down the swap device while it
> > still contains active swapped-out pages?
>
> Yes, this is a bug, but it's already a bug for existing physical swap
> devices IIUC.

existing bug from before this patch series.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham
reviews.summary: Clarified that the range of swap devices never reaches the high bit in real systems, and that this is an existing bug from before the patch series.
reviews.raw_body:

> inuse_page is atomic_long_t already, but we use the 30th bit, which
> contradicts comment "Use the second highest bit of inuse_pages
> counter". It's only incidentally correct because the range of the swap
> device never reaches this high.

to clarify a bit - I meant to say the range of the swap devices never
reach this high in real system that I have seen, not that it's not

[...]

> > Could this cause try_to_unuse() to abort prematurely thinking the device is
> > empty, causing sys_swapoff() to forcefully tear down the swap device while it

>

existing bug from before this patch series.

2026-06-14.patch_summary: Patch series to widen swap_info_struct max/pages to unsigned long, addressing existing bugs and inconsistencies in the code.

2026-06-15.patch_summary: Patch series to widen swap_info_struct max/pages to unsigned long, addressing existing bugs and inconsistencies in the code.
